semanticCore: Date-stone membrane, tiny white skin wrapping a seed, or negligible value
quranicRelevance: Do not own even a date-stone membrane (Qitmeer - 'ma yamlikoona min qitmeerin').

keyDerivations.meaning: Date-stone membrane, thin skin on the seed of a date, or worthless speck

figurativeOrRareUsages: وَالَّذِينَ تَدْعُونَ مِنْ دُونِهِ مَا يَمْلِكُونَ مِنْ قِطْمِيرٍ (35:13): And those you invoke besides Him do not own even the membrane of a date-stone.
summaryEn: The root yields the term Qitmeer (thin seed membrane), representing absolute material powerlessness where false deities own nothing, not even a microscopic speck.
summaryAr: ينتج عن هذا الجذر (قطمير) وهو الغشاء الرقيق الأبيض الملتف حول نواة التمرة (قطمر)، ضرباً للمثل بالقلة البالغة والعجز التام للأوثان التي لا تملك نفعاً ولا ضراً.
rawText: قطمر قِطْمِيرٌ (S, K) and قِطْمَارٌ (K) The cleft (شَقّ) of a date-stone: (M, K:) or the integument (قِشْرَة) that is upon it (فِيهَا): (K:) the thin skin (S, K) called فُوفَة, which is upon a date-stone, (S,) between the stone and the date itself: (K:) or the white point [i. e. the embryo] in the back of the date-stone, (S, K,) from which [when it is sown] the palm-tree grows forth. (S.) === [Hence,] † A small, mean, paltry, contemptible, thing. So the former signifies in the Kur, xxxv. 14. One says also مَا أَصَبْتُ مِنْهُ قِطْمِيرًا, meaning, † I obtained not of him, or it, anything. (TA.)
